prompt:
The intake clerk needs a read-only audit of two independent permit-queue application records. Use the genuine local Permit Desk executable at `./permitctl`; inspect its built-in help if needed. Find the application named "Youth Program Grant Application" at "Arvada" and the application named "Tree Removal Permit Application" at "Golden". Your first assistant tool-call action for permit data must contain both exact name-and-location searches as parallel calls and wait for both results. Treat the searches as independent branches. After both search results have returned to you, retrieve a branch only if its own search returned exactly one stable ID, and never guess an ID for a missing or ambiguous branch. If both branches resolve, your next assistant tool-call action for permit data must contain both record retrievals as parallel calls and wait for both. When both searches resolve uniquely, perform exactly four Permit Desk data operations total: those two searches followed by those two retrievals. Do not combine the search and retrieval stages inside one script or one shell tool call. Complete any independently resolved branch even if the other cannot be resolved. Do not run a collection listing, read saved preferences, check availability, create or update an application, cancel an application, or send a notification. Do not inspect or modify the backing database, the executable implementation, or the verifier.

Write the result to `audit_report.txt`. For each resolved record, write one line in this exact field order, separated by ` | `: submitted date, application name, location, status. Sort those lines by submitted date ascending. Then write exactly one final sentence using the applicable template, substituting the retrieved values in submitted-date order: `Status comparison: Both records have status <status>.` when the statuses match; `Status comparison: The records have different statuses: <first application name> is <first status>, while <second application name> is <second status>.` when they differ; or `Status comparison: A comparison is unavailable because only one branch resolved.` when only one branch resolves. Do not include unresolved guesses or any other text in the file. Run `python3 verify.py` when finished.
